other_observers: 17July Joanie Kleypas 25July Bob Zilly, Peter Gent, Gary Matthews; yes with Joanie Kleypas
specific_location: 50-100 m below Calypso Cascades in the Wild Basin section of Rocky Mountain National Park. This is just below the second bridge on this trail.
description: Peter Gent was the only person who saw this bird. He said it was not dark enough to be a Pacific Wren and it had flecking in the throat.  Gender is based on the fact that this bird was singing in July in suitable breeding habitat.Behaviors: The bird was singing along a 50 m stretch of high mountain stream in July. There were numerous large downed trees and heavy underbrush.Call: The identity is mainly based on recordings of this bird's song. It was more musical than the song of Pacific Wren.Plumage: not observed
